seo.title: CRM & Sales Pipeline Setup — One Inbox, Every Lead | AQ Marketing
seo.description: A small-business CRM with a kanban pipeline, auto-assignment, follow-up tasks, and full lead history. Pulls from web, phone, chat, email, and ads into one source of truth. Wired to your existing tools.

sections.heading: Every lead. One pipeline. No more dropped follow-ups.
sections.lede: A real CRM with a kanban pipeline that pulls every inbound lead — web form, phone, chat, email, Google ad, social — into one inbox. Auto-assigns to the right person, schedules follow-ups, and shows you the deals about to slip. So no $4,000 quote sits in someone's email for two weeks.

ctas.label: See it on a sample team

sections.heading: What a unified pipeline does for your follow-up.
sections.intro: When every lead lives in one place with a required next action and timestamps, follow-up goes from "sometimes" to "always."

stats.label: Every lead in one place, not scattered across tools
Each lead carries a required-by date so nothing stalls
Auto-assignment plus alerts when a lead goes untouched
No more leads lost to "I thought you had it"

sections.heading: Why your team keeps losing deals to "oh, I missed that one."
sections.intro: The leads aren't lost because your team is bad. They're lost because the system is six tools, three inboxes, and a sticky note on someone's desk.

cards.title: Leads scattered across 6 tools
cards.body: Web forms in Gmail, calls in voicemail, chats in Slack, ads in Facebook lead-gen, walk-ins on a notepad, referrals in a text thread. No source of truth.

cards.title: Follow-up rot
cards.body: Day 1 reply, day 3 "will follow up," day 14 forgot. NSI Research: 48% of small-business leads get only one follow-up touch. They book with the second person who tries.

cards.title: "I thought YOU were on it"
cards.body: Two team members both think the other is replying. Or neither is. Either way the customer is gone — and so is the chance to learn from it.

cards.title: No visibility for the owner
cards.body: Owner can't tell which deals are stuck, which need pricing approval, which got ghosted. Decisions made on vibes, not data.

sections.heading: What we wire into the pipeline.

items.title: Multi-source intake
items.body: Web forms, phone (via call tracking + AI receptionist), chat, email, Facebook lead ads, Google ads form extensions, walk-ins. Single inbox.

items.title: Auto-assignment + SLA
items.body: Round-robin or rules-based (by service, ZIP, or load). 15-minute SLA alerts if a lead hasn't been touched. Owner-visible breach dashboard.

items.title: Required next-action
items.body: Every lead in pipeline has a required-by date for the next action. Misses trigger an alert. "In progress" with no action for 7+ days is impossible.

items.title: Templates + sequences
items.body: Pre-built email + SMS templates for common touches. 3-touch follow-up sequences for unanswered leads run automatically.

items.title: Owner dashboard
items.body: Pipeline by stage, value, age. Stuck deals. SLA breaches. Win rate by source. Forecast — what's likely to close this month.

items.title: Integrates with your stack
items.body: Pulls from Jobber, HCP, ServiceTitan, Calendly, Google Workspace, Outlook, Twilio, plus webhook anything. Doesn't replace your dispatch tool — augments it.

sections.heading: From signed to live, with your team trained.

steps.title: Audit + select
steps.body: Inventory your current lead sources, tools, and team workflow. Pick the right CRM (HubSpot, Pipedrive, Close, Zoho, GHL — depends on size + budget).

steps.title: Wire + import
steps.body: Pipeline stages built. Lead routing rules. Existing leads imported. Email/SMS templates loaded. SLA + alerts configured.

steps.title: Train
steps.body: Live training with each team member. Quick-reference cards. Slack/Teams integration so leads ping in chat where you live.

steps.title: Tune
steps.body: First 30 days of leads inform: stage names, follow-up cadence, SLA times, dashboard layout. We adjust before stepping back.

sections.heading: Pick the right CRM, then make it actually used.
sections.prose_html:
The CRM tool itself matters less than how it's set up and whether the team uses it. We've watched HubSpot, Pipedrive, Close, Zoho, GoHighLevel, and a dozen others all succeed and fail in the same business. The difference is configuration: clean stages, required fields, auto-assignment, real alerts, and a dashboard the owner actually checks.

Tool selection by team size: 1–3 people, GoHighLevel or Close (cheap, simple). 4–10 people, Pipedrive or HubSpot Starter. 10+ or scaling fast, HubSpot Sales Hub Pro. We're tool-agnostic — we recommend the cheapest one that solves your problem and grows with you.

The 15-minute SLA is the leverage point

InsideSales / HBR data: leads contacted within 5 minutes are 21× more likely to close than leads contacted after 30 minutes. We set the SLA at 15 minutes during business hours (and route to AI after-hours). Misses page the team. Owner sees the breach dashboard. Two weeks of this and the team's habit changes.

Pricing

Software cost depends on the CRM you pick — many start free, with premium tiers up to about $90 per user/month. Our setup and management is part of your Smart Website & Marketing Package at $500/mo; we confirm the exact scope at your free audit. The aim is simple: stop losing deals to dropped follow-ups so the system pays for itself.

aside_items.note_body: 1–3 people: GoHighLevel/Close. 4–10: Pipedrive/HubSpot Starter. 10+: HubSpot Sales Hub Pro. We're tool-agnostic — cheapest that solves the problem.
InsideSales/HBR: leads contacted within 5 min are 21× more likely to close vs. 30+ min. We set SLA at 15 min during business hours.
Part of our Smart Website & Marketing Package at $500/mo (a 12-month agreement) — included in or added to your plan. We confirm the exact scope for your business at your free audit.

items.a: We're tool-agnostic. Recommendations by size: 1–3 people GoHighLevel or Close; 4–10 Pipedrive or HubSpot Starter; 10+ HubSpot Sales Hub Pro. If you already have a CRM, we'll work with it — no migration unless it's clearly the wrong fit.

items.q: Will it replace my dispatch tool (Jobber, HCP, ServiceTitan)?
items.a: No — it augments. The CRM handles pre-job (lead → quote → close). Your dispatch tool handles post-close (job → schedule → invoice). They share data via integration.

The CRM software itself is third-party ($0–$90 per user/month depending on tier). Our setup and management is part of your Smart Website & Marketing Package at $500/mo (a 12-month agreement) — we confirm the exact scope at your free audit.

items.q: Will my team actually use it?
items.a: That's the real question. We design the workflow so it's less work than the current chaos — required fields are minimal, lead intake is automatic, follow-ups are templated. The simpler the daily routine, the more the team sticks with it.

items.q: Can it pull from my call tracking?
items.a: Yes — every call from CallRail, CallTrackingMetrics, or our AI receptionist hits the CRM as a lead with the recording attached and source tagged.
Owner dashboard built day-1: pipeline by stage and value, SLA breaches, stuck deals, win rate by source, monthly forecast. Weekly auto-emailed digest summarizing the dashboard.

cards.title: AI Chatbot
cards.body: Every chat lead lands in pipeline tagged Hot/Warm/Cold with the full transcript. No copy-paste, no lost context.

cards.title: Email & SMS
cards.body: Pipeline stage drives messaging. "Quoted but no answer" gets a different sequence than "long-time customer."

cards.title: Reporting Dashboard
cards.body: Roll up pipeline data into the unified reporting dashboard alongside SEO, ads, and reviews. One owner view, every channel.

sections.heading: See where your leads are leaking — by stage, by source, by person.
sections.body: Free 30-minute working session: we map your current lead flow, count the dropouts, and show you the 2–3 leverage points where pipeline rollout will pay back fastest.

stats.label: Working session, your real lead flow
Tool-agnostic — we work with what's right
